title: Deguelia marginata
intro: Deguelia marginata, also known as the "Marginal Deguelia," is a plant species in the family Leguminosae, native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Deguelia marginata include isoflavones, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Deguelia marginata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in infusions and decoctions, to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Deguelia marginata is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or pea family. It belongs to the genus Deguelia, which consists of several species of flowering plants found in tropical regions. The plant is native to tropical Africa and is commonly found in the wild. Deguelia marginata is classified as a climber, meaning it grows by twining around other plants or structures for support. The exact classification of Deguelia marginata at the species level is not well-documented, but it is known to be closely related to other species within the Deguelia genus.
